claim_text: GPAI obligations under Articles 53–55 entered application on 2 August 2025, with systemic-risk provisions applying to models above the 10^25 FLOPs threshold.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: The Article 2(5g) open-source exemption excludes free and open-source GPAI models from several transparency obligations unless they meet the systemic-risk threshold, in which case the exemption does not apply.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: The European AI Office's Code of Practice was published on 2025-07-10 and operationalizes Article 53 transparency requirements including the sufficiently-detailed-summary of training data.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Article 53 imposes on GPAI providers the obligation to publish a sufficiently detailed summary of training data, maintain technical documentation, and cooperate with downstream deployers.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: The GPAI provisions apply to any general-purpose AI model placed on the EU market, regardless of where the provider is established.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: The AI Office issued its first GPAI guidance letters in Q1 2026, opening the enforcement phase for the transparency obligations.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: The European Parliament's research service expects the Commission to open formal proceedings against at least one GPAI provider before the end of 2026.
label: PROBABLY TRUE
notes: Single Tier 1 source, uncorroborated — credibility 2 per the §4.1 cascade (supporting_Tier1 ≥ 1, no contradictor). Inline-tagged in the main body; excluded from the executive summary.

claim_text: Article 2(5g) exempts free and open-source GPAI models from transparency requirements of Articles 53(1)(a) and (b) only when parameters, architecture details, and usage information are publicly available.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: The Article 2(5g) exemption is void for any model meeting the systemic-risk threshold of 10^25 FLOPs; those models remain subject to the full Article 55 obligations regardless of licensing.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Comparative analysis finds the EU's open-source carve-out narrower than the regulatory treatment of open models in the US and UK frameworks.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Meta has positioned Llama as compliant under Article 2(5g), Mistral has issued a European regulatory alignment statement, and HuggingFace released model-card templates aligned to the Code of Practice.
label: CONFIRMED
notes: Three independent Tier 2 provider statements, no contradictor — credibility 1 per the §4.1 cascade (supporting_Tier12 ≥ 2).

claim_text: Press coverage of the first enforcement letters reported no immediate sanctions against open-source providers.
label: CONFIRMED

claim_text: Mistral's alignment statement commits to publishing training-data summaries beyond the exemption's minimum requirements.
label: POSSIBLY TRUE
notes: Single Tier 2 source, uncorroborated — credibility 3 per the §4.1 cascade (supporting_Tier12 = 1, no contradictor). Inline-tagged in the main body.

claim_text: The scope of "sufficiently detailed summary" of training data under Article 53(1)(d) remains disputed: the Commission's July 2025 template is read by Meta as compatible with existing Llama disclosures, and by Mozilla as requiring substantially more granular dataset provenance than any current open-source release provides.
label: CONFIRMED
notes: Meta-claim about the existence and shape of the dispute — confirmed by all three sources. The competing readings themselves are presented side by side, unresolved; expected to resolve after the Q3 2026 Code of Practice clarifications (S011).

claim_text: Compliance costs exceed €1M for small open-source GPAI providers.
label: UNVERIFIED
notes: Only Tier 3 support — credibility 6 per the §4.1 cascade. Follow-up required from Gartner/McKinsey or Commission impact assessment.
